primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité en comptes consolidés d’un dividende intragroupe comptabilisé en créance à recevoir comme élément couvert au titre du risque de change, et identification du modèle de comptabilité de couverture pertinent sous IFRS 9.

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the strict precedence rule that IFRS supersedes IAS for overlapping hedge accounting eligibility and model questions, so IFRS 9 governs this issue and IAS 39 is discarded because the question is not about the IAS 39 portfolio fair value hedge exception.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IFRS 9 directly addresses eligible hedged items in consolidated financial statements and the three hedge accounting models, while IAS 39 remains relevant only for a specific portfolio fair value hedge exception not implicated by the question.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern what can be a hedged item in consolidated financial statements and identify the available hedge accounting models.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify how foreign exchange differences arise on monetary items and how hedge accounting interacts with IAS 21 in consolidated financial statements.
This Interpretation constrains and clarifies the separate net investment hedge model, which is one of the named hedge accounting families relevant to foreign currency risk.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs concern hedge accounting disclosures rather than identifying the governing hedge accounting approaches.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting models for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies fair value hedge as a distinct named hedge accounting model for exposures to changes in fair value of recognised items attributable to a particular risk.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct named hedge accounting model for variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk, including forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a separate named hedge accounting model for foreign currency exposure linked to a net investment.
